title: CORONAVIRUSES: PATHOGENESIS, TRANSMISSION, AND CONTROL STRATEGIES
author: Mahajan Aditya Dhondiba,Togzukova ZH
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Coronaviruses are enveloped, positive-sense, single-stranded RNA viruses causing illnesses from common cold to severe respiratory syndromes like MERS, SARS, and COVID-19. They are zoonotic and capable of crossing species barriers, leading to global outbreaks like the COVID-19 pandemic. Understanding their structure, transmission, and control measures is essential.
steps_raw: Coronaviruses are enveloped, positive-sense, single-stranded RNA viruses causing illnesses from common cold to severe respiratory syndromes like MERS, SARS, and COVID-19.
